sentence: Hoije nun stá friu, mas manhana bai a chober.
ipa: ˈojʒɨ ˈnũ ˈs̺ta ˈfɾju ˈmas̺ mɐˈɲanɐ ˈbaj ɐ tʃuˈβeɾ
gloss_en: Today it isn't cold, but tomorrow it's going to rain.
notes: Unstressed reduction to [ɨ ɐ u] and intervocalic /b/-spirantisation chober->[tʃuˈβeɾ], nasal [ũ] (mateus_dandrade2000).

sentence: Un quilo de queiso custa uito euros.
ipa: ˈũ ˈkilu dɨ ˈkejz̺u ˈkus̺tɐ ˈujtu ˈewɾus̺
gloss_en: A kilo of cheese costs eight euros.
notes: Standard Mirandese lexicon; the ⟨ei⟩ diphthong is retained in queiso->[ˈkejz̺u] (convencao1999).

sentence: Bou a l doutor l lhunes a la tarde.
ipa: ˈbow ɐ l doˈwtoɾ l ˈʎunɨs̺ ɐ lɐ ˈtaɾdɨ
gloss_en: I'm going to the doctor on Monday afternoon.
notes: Leonese word-initial ⟨l⟩->[ʎ] palatalisation in lhunes->[ˈʎunɨs̺] (vasconcelos1900).

sentence: La mie família bibe nua casa an Miranda.
ipa: lɐ ˈmjɛ fɐˈmilja ˈbiβɨ ˈnwa ˈkaz̺ɐ ɐ̃ miˈɾandɐ
gloss_en: My family lives in a house in Miranda.
notes: Nasal digraph ⟨an⟩->[ɐ̃] before a consonant in an/Miranda; heavy unstressed reduction (mateus_dandrade2000).

sentence: Bai a la dreita i passa la punte.
ipa: ˈbaj ɐ lɐ ˈdɾejtɐ i ˈpas̺ɐ lɐ ˈpũtɨ
gloss_en: Go to the right and cross the bridge.
notes: Imperative-register directions; the ⟨ei⟩ diphthong is kept in dreita->[ˈdɾejtɐ] (convencao1999).

sentence: Este pan mirandés stá mui buôno.
ipa: ˈes̺tɨ ˈpɐ̃ miɾɐˈndɛs̺ ˈs̺ta ˈmuj ˈbwɔnu
gloss_en: This Mirandese bread is very good.
notes: Preserved Leonese rising diphthong ⟨uô⟩->[wɔ] in buôno->[ˈbwɔnu] (vasconcelos1900).

sentence: Adonde stá la eigreija de l pobo?
ipa: ɐˈdondɨ ˈs̺ta lɐ eˈjɡɾejʒɐ dɨ l ˈpoβu
gloss_en: Where is the village church?
notes: Church/village lexicon; ⟨ei⟩ in eigreija->[eˈjɡɾejʒɐ] and the apico-alveolar sibilant series (convencao1999).

sentence: Quantos ninos hai na scuola?
ipa: ˈkwɐ̃tus̺ ˈninus̺ ˈaj nɐ ˈs̺kwolɐ
gloss_en: How many children are there at the school?
notes: Unstressed reduction and the ⟨an⟩ nasal vowel in quantos->[ˈkɐ̃tus̺] (mateus_dandrade2000).

sentence: Se chobir manhana, nun bamos a l campo.
ipa: s̺ɨ tʃuˈβiɾ mɐˈɲanɐ ˈnũ ˈbamus̺ ɐ l ˈkɐ̃pu
gloss_en: If it rains tomorrow, we won't go to the field.
notes: Conditional register; intervocalic /b/-spirantisation chobir->[tʃuˈβiɾ] and nasal nun (mateus_dandrade2000).

sentence: Onte mercéi bino i queiso na tenda.
ipa: ˈõtɨ mɨˈɾsɛi ˈbinu i ˈkejz̺u nɐ ˈtendɐ
gloss_en: Yesterday I bought wine and cheese at the shop.
notes: Past-tense register; the ⟨ei⟩ diphthong in queiso, mercéi (vasconcelos1900).

sentence: Para l anho que ben bamos a Pertual.
ipa: ˈpaɾɐ l ˈaɲu kɨ ˈbẽ ˈbamus̺ ɐ pɨˈɾtwal
gloss_en: Next year we are going to Portugal.
notes: Palatal ⟨nh⟩->[ɲ] in anho->[ˈaɲu] and the nasal monophthong ben->[ˈbẽ] (mateus_dandrade2000).

sentence: Chamou-me la mie armana pul telifone.
ipa: ˈtʃamow mɨ lɐ ˈmjɛ ɐˈɾmanɐ pul tɨliˈfonɨ
gloss_en: My sister phoned me.
notes: Regional everyday lexicon armana, telifone attested for the variety (ferreira1995).

sentence: Buonos dies, cumo stás? Stou bien, oubrigado.
ipa: ˈbwonus̺ ˈdjɛs̺ ˈkumu ˈs̺tas̺ ˈs̺tow ˈbjẽ owbɾiˈɣadu
gloss_en: Good morning, how are you? I'm well, thank you.
notes: Greeting formulae; the ⟨ou⟩ diphthong is retained in stou, oubrigado (convencao1999).

sentence: Quien nun trabalha, nun cume.
ipa: ˈkjɛn ˈnũ tɾɐˈβaʎɐ ˈnũ ˈkumɨ
gloss_en: He who doesn't work, doesn't eat.
notes: Proverb register; palatal lateral ⟨lh⟩->[ʎ] in trabalha->[tɾɐˈβaʎɐ] (ferreira1995).

sentence: La raposa cuôrre pula carreira arriba.
ipa: lɐ rɐˈpoz̺ɐ ˈkwɔrɨ pulɐ kɐˈrejɾɐ ɐˈriβɐ
gloss_en: The fox runs up the track.
notes: Trill /r/ in carreira->[kɐˈrejɾɐ] and the ⟨uô⟩ diphthong in cuôrre; fox lexicon raposa (vasconcelos1900).

sentence: Bi ua streilha i la lhuna ne l cielo.
ipa: ˈbi ˈwa ˈs̺tɾejʎɐ i lɐ ˈʎunɐ nɨ l ˈsjɛlu
gloss_en: I saw a star and the moon in the sky.
notes: Word-initial ⟨lh⟩/⟨l⟩->[ʎ] in lhuna and the ⟨ei⟩ diphthong in streilha (vasconcelos1900).

sentence: L sol calece la tierra al meidie.
ipa: l ˈs̺ol kɐˈlesɨ lɐ ˈtjɛrɐ ˈal ˈmejdjɛ
gloss_en: The sun warms the earth at midday.
notes: Leonese ⟨ie⟩->[jɛ] with trill /r/ in tierra->[ˈtjɛrɐ]; ⟨ei⟩ in meidie (vasconcelos1900).

sentence: La mulhier apanha las fuolhas de l castanheiro.
ipa: lɐ muˈʎjɛɾ ɐˈpaɲɐ lɐs̺ ˈfwoʎɐs̺ dɨ l kɐs̺tɐˈɲejɾu
gloss_en: The woman gathers the leaves of the chestnut tree.
notes: Palatal ⟨lh⟩->[ʎ] (mulhier, fuolhas) and ⟨nh⟩->[ɲ] (apanha); ⟨uô⟩ diphthong in fuolhas (vasconcelos1900).

sentence: La puôrta de l huôrto stá abierta.
ipa: lɐ ˈpwɔɾtɐ dɨ l ˈwɔɾtu ˈs̺ta ɐˈβjɛɾtɐ
gloss_en: The door of the vegetable garden is open.
notes: Preserved Leonese ⟨uô⟩->[wɔ] in puôrta, huôrto (vasconcelos1900).

sentence: L campo stá cheno de flores i de yerba.
ipa: l ˈkɐ̃pu ˈs̺ta ˈtʃenu dɨ ˈfloɾɨs̺ i dɨ ˈjeɾbɐ
gloss_en: The field is full of flowers and grass.
notes: Nasal ⟨an⟩->[ɐ̃] in campo and the ⟨ye⟩ onset in yerba->[ˈjeɾbɐ] (convencao1999).
